hero.name: Abdul Haseeb
hero.description: I craft production-ready agentic AI systems, custom voice agents, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) applications with clean architecture, questionable sleep habits, and an amount of caffeine that mildly worries my doctor.

about.title: About
about.subtitle: Me
paragraphs: Hi, I'm **Abdul Haseeb** — an AI Engineer who builds production-ready agentic architectures, RAG systems, and low-latency AI integrations, usually while telling myself 'just one more commit and then I'll sleep.'

My journey started with full-stack web development and somehow escalated into *agentic workflows, AI voice agents, and semantic context-retrieval pipelines* — a plot twist nobody, including me, saw coming.
Whether it's a sleek UI or a stubborn backend, I enjoy turning caffeine and vague requirements into real, impactful products through clean, efficient code that occasionally works on the first try.

history.title: Madrasa-tul-Madina
history.description: Hifz-e-Quran Certification with Tajweed and Islamic Studies

history.title: DAR-UL-MADINAH
history.description: Matriculation in Computer Science, where I first discovered that computers say 'no' far more often than 'yes.'

history.title: Saylani Mass IT Training
history.description: Professional certification in MERN Stack with hands-on projects, and the lasting discovery that 3 AM is peak productivity.

history.title: Bachelor of Computer Science (BSCS)
history.description: Bachelor's in Computer Science — four years of algorithms, all-nighters, and confidently nodding through Big-O questions.

history.title: AI Engineer
history.description: Designed and deployed production-ready AI voice agents that talk to users so I don't have to. Architected the complete backend and semantic context-retrieval layer for a healthcare application, wiring up secure data pipelines that update the knowledge base faster than I update my sleep schedule. Engineered low-latency asynchronous systems handling 75–90 complex interactions per week, cutting API response times to under 2 minutes — down from 'please hold, contemplating existence.' Owned the end-to-end product lifecycle, from data structuring and system prompt design to secure API deployment and cross-platform frontend integration, powered entirely by deadlines and dangerous amounts of espresso.

history.title: Product Engineer
history.description: Owned end-to-end frontend delivery for ShipTag, a Saudi logistics company — built 40+ screens from Figma to production using React.js, Tailwind CSS, and HeroUI, translating designer dreams into pixels without crying (much). Shipped a fully responsive UI within deadline, and the client approved it with zero revision requests — a rare event I've mentally framed on my wall.

skills.title: Technical
skills.subtitle: Skills
skills.description: A comprehensive list of technologies and tools I use to build modern software — and occasionally argue with at 2 AM.

projects.title: Featured
projects.subtitle: Projects
projects.description: Here are some projects I've built, ranging from SaaS platforms to AI healthcare assistants — each one lovingly shipped, and only mildly haunted by past bugs.

items.title: ShipTag
items.description: End-to-end merchant portal for a shipping SaaS — helping e-commerce businesses across Saudi Arabia and beyond ship things, so customers can angrily refresh the tracking page in real time.
items.longDescription: Built a comprehensive merchant portal for a logistics SaaS. Integrated multi-carrier shipping APIs (UPS, FedEx, DHL, Aramex) for real-time rate comparison, shipping label generation (PDF/ZPL), and automated order sync with platforms like Shopify and WooCommerce — basically taught four rival carriers to sit at the same table without a fight.

caseStudy.overview: ShipTag is a centralized web portal built to simplify global shipping for digital merchants and reduce the number of 'where is my package?!' emails.
caseStudy.objective: To aggregate multiple shipping carrier services into one high-performance dashboard UI, instead of ten browser tabs and a prayer.
caseStudy.solution: Developed custom REST API integration flows with automated shipping label pipelines that handle the boring parts so humans don't have to.
caseStudy.challenges: Managing asynchronous tracking synchronization across carrier APIs that each speak a slightly different dialect of chaos.
caseStudy.results: Successfully streamlined shipment handling, cutting manual processing time by 80% — the other 20% is just admiring the dashboard.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: LeadFlow AI
items.description: Intelligent lead scoring pipeline using OpenAI LLMs and async data routing to judge your leads faster, and far less emotionally, than the sales team.
items.longDescription: Engineered an enterprise-grade AI lead intelligence agent using advanced prompt engineering and multi-agent decision logic on OpenAI models. The system ingests dynamic webhooks, runs real-time semantic screening and cognitive scoring (0-100) with GPT-4o-mini, and orchestrates tri-conditional routing. High-intent 'hot leads' instantly become strategic HubSpot deals and fire off multi-channel Slack alerts — so no promising customer quietly dies of neglect in an inbox.

caseStudy.overview: LeadFlow AI is an autonomous ingestion pipeline built for high-scale enterprise lead screening — think bouncer, but for your CRM.
caseStudy.objective: To accurately evaluate chaotic user intent with real-time models, so nobody has to read 400 form submissions by hand.
caseStudy.solution: Deployed webhook-driven backend infrastructure parsing structured data through OpenAI LLM blocks, with just enough guardrails to keep the AI honest.
caseStudy.challenges: Convincing the AI to stop hallucinating creative new JSON formats under heavy input load.
caseStudy.results: Achieved a 25% lift in pipeline conversion with seamless dynamic routing, and zero leads lost to the void.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: OrderSync AI
items.description: Event-driven supply chain engine powered by cloud webhooks — automating logistics so orders fulfill themselves while you sleep (guilt-free).
items.longDescription: Engineered an autonomous, event-driven order processing and communications pipeline. The engine processes real-time Shopify transactions, runs algorithmic inventory checks, talks to the ShipEngine API for cloud-based logistics docs, and nudges customers via WhatsApp and transactional email. Out-of-stock variations trigger polite exception-handling instead of a full-blown panic.

caseStudy.overview: OrderSync AI is an event-driven automation framework built for supply chain sync — the responsible adult your fulfillment process never had.
caseStudy.objective: To abstract away complex manual logistics fulfillment across multiple vendor systems, and the migraines that come with it.
caseStudy.solution: Architected a highly responsive webhook router wiring Shopify nodes straight into logistics APIs, no human clicking required.
caseStudy.challenges: Configuring reliable circuit breakers to handle inventory stockout loops before they spiral into infinite sadness.
caseStudy.results: Successfully automated 100% of the order lifecycle, eliminating processing blocks and one very stressed operations manager.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: PricePulse AI
items.description: Autonomous market intelligence agent that scrapes competitors and quietly judges their pricing, so you always win the margin game.
items.longDescription: Developed an autonomous market intelligence agent combining scheduled background workers, distributed web scraping, and GPT-4 brains. It grabs raw competitor price tables daily, spots pricing tricks and market anomalies, updates e-commerce databases via custom APIs, and drops the gossip straight into your operational Slack.

caseStudy.overview: PricePulse AI is an autonomous scraping framework built for real-time retail intelligence — a stalker, but the legal, profitable kind.
caseStudy.objective: To continuously track shifting competitor pricing around the clock without a human ever refreshing a page.
caseStudy.solution: Built a scheduled cron worker engine leveraging custom GPT analytics to parse messy tables into clean insights.
caseStudy.challenges: Handling dynamic single-page applications that really, really didn't want to be scraped.
caseStudy.results: Optimized revenue capture, increasing targeted item margins by 8% — small number, big smug energy.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: Luxury Studio
items.description: Complete e-commerce storefront for a clothing brand with filtering, search, and a checkout that actually wants to take your money.
items.longDescription: A high-performance e-commerce platform built with React and Vite. Features advanced product filtering, category navigation, a smooth cart experience, and a responsive UI that looks good on everything from a flagship phone to a device from 2013.

caseStudy.overview: Luxury Studio is a high-performance storefront designed for digital apparel brands with taste and impatience.
caseStudy.objective: To build a mobile checkout funnel so smooth users forget they're spending real money.
caseStudy.solution: Leveraged decoupled state management and responsive styling to keep everything fast and pretty.
caseStudy.challenges: Shrinking asset payloads so mobile users don't rage-quit during load.
caseStudy.results: Achieved an excellent 98 Lighthouse score and boosted retention — the loading spinner rarely gets stage time anymore.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: Luxury Admin
items.description: Full-featured admin panel for end-to-end store management — inventory, orders, and analytics, minus the spreadsheet-induced tears.
items.longDescription: A comprehensive management dashboard for the Luxury Studio platform. Admins can manage products, track inventory in real time, process orders, and stare at detailed sales analytics with a coffee firmly in hand.

caseStudy.overview: Luxury Admin is the control room where retail managers press buttons and feel powerful.
caseStudy.objective: To centralize heavy inventory tracking and order metrics into one place, not seventeen tabs.
caseStudy.solution: Engineered a secure data dashboard syncing storage with sub-second API listeners, fast enough to feel like magic.
caseStudy.challenges: Keeping the layout calm while rendering deeply nested product options that had commitment issues.
caseStudy.results: Accelerated admin work by 40% and removed catalog lag, and possibly a few gray hairs.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: ADM Portal

items.longDescription: Ads Management System (ADM) is a premium reporting platform for marketing agencies. It centralizes cross-channel performance data into clean dashboards, enabling deep-dive intelligence and KPI tracking that doesn't require a decoder ring.

caseStudy.overview: ADM Portal is a dynamic analytics engine that herds scattered channel marketing metrics into one calm room.
caseStudy.objective: To turn many messy raw tracking endpoints into a few clean visual feeds.
caseStudy.solution: Built an elegant data aggregation framework on scalable document databases that scale better than my patience.
caseStudy.challenges: Managing overlapping heavy analytical payloads while keeping request times low and tempers lower.
caseStudy.results: Cut weekly tracking time in half for agencies, boosting client retention and reducing 'can you re-send that report?' pings.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.title: AdPulse
items.description: A robust, industry-ready ad management platform with microservices, Docker, and enough best practices to make senior engineers nod approvingly.
items.longDescription: AdPulse is a high-performance advertising management ecosystem. Not a simple UI toy — it runs a distributed microservices architecture, real-time data sync with Socket.io, and containerized services using Docker, following industry standards for security, scalability, and general showing off.

caseStudy.overview: AdPulse is a distributed microservices platform engineered for production ad monitoring, and for flexing at meetups.
caseStudy.objective: To deploy an event-driven system that tracks channel state the instant it changes.
caseStudy.solution: Implemented containerized microservices with decoupled relational storage, each service minding its own business.
caseStudy.challenges: Getting websocket connections to sync smoothly across separate backends that didn't want to talk.
caseStudy.results: Delivered sub-100ms responses with automated alerting, and a container for basically everything.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.longDescription: CareSync bridges the gap between patients and doctors with symptom checking, appointment scheduling, and secure medical record storage — a waiting room, minus the ancient magazines.

caseStudy.overview: CareSync is an intelligent medical coordinator designed to make clinic intake less painful than the actual visit.

caseStudy.challenges: Meeting strict data protection standards without adding friction that makes staff want to scream.

metrics.label: Cups of Coffee During This Project

items.longDescription: A modern workspace where teams can chat, share files, and hold video calls, with AI assistants in every channel to help with research and drafting — the coworker who never takes lunch and never steals your food.

items.longDescription: CryptoPulse tracks your crypto assets across exchanges and uses AI for sentiment analysis and trend predictions — financial advice not included, emotional support charts very much included.

caseStudy.overview: CryptoPulse is an analytical portfolio manager capturing real-time token trends before your anxiety does.
caseStudy.objective: To unify multiple fragmented blockchain feeds into one clean view instead of ten open exchanges.
caseStudy.solution: Deployed client-side predictive engines synced with high-speed ticker systems.
caseStudy.challenges: Normalizing continuous pricing vectors streaming from exchanges that update at wildly different vibes.
caseStudy.results: Delivered measurable lift on portfolio returns via cleaner signals — past performance not a guarantee, obviously.

items.longDescription: Finance Assistant helps users take control of their money. Link accounts, track every penny, and get AI-driven insights on saving more — the friend who says 'do you really need that?' but with actual data.

caseStudy.overview: FinSmart AI is a secure budgeting system that automates your balance records and mild financial guilt.
caseStudy.objective: To link multi-institution accounts cleanly under one tidy, private roof.

items.longDescription: Hireflow simplifies the hiring process. Candidates apply for jobs while employers manage applications and schedule interviews through an interface intuitive enough for even the most spreadsheet-averse recruiter.

caseStudy.overview: Meridian Shop is a structural dashboard engineering live sales transaction monitoring.

items.title: Shrinkly

items.longDescription: Modern URL shortener with support for custom slugs, link expiration, and user authentication. Built for reliability, security, and a clean UX using the MERN stack — big links go in, tiny links come out, everyone's happy.

caseStudy.overview: Shrinkly is a link management application routing production link redirection traffic without dropping the ball.
caseStudy.objective: To offer fast link abbreviation plus proper analytics, not just a random string generator.
caseStudy.solution: Built a specialized hashing manager writing to a document store, quick and collision-shy.
caseStudy.challenges: Validating custom slug allocations under concurrent requests so two users don't fight over 'coollink.'
caseStudy.results: Ensured highly reliable link resolution with zero tracking lag — links that actually go where you promised.

items.longDescription: A feature-rich employee management system built with the MERN stack. Includes task assignment, role-based login (admin/user), performance tracking, and a responsive dashboard interface — basically HR paperwork, but it doesn't hurt.

reviews.title: Client
reviews.subtitle: Reviews
reviews.description: Hear from brave people who've collaborated with me and lived to review it.

items.name: James Anderson
items.review: Working with Haseeb was a fantastic experience. He delivered exactly what we needed, on time, with clean code — and somehow never once said 'it works on my machine.'

items.name: Emily Stone
items.review: Very reliable and professional. Haseeb understood our requirements clearly and exceeded expectations — I'm fairly sure he runs on caffeine and pure competence.

items.name: Ahmed Khalid
items.review: Great communication and impressive React skills. He built us a secure, fast web app, and the only bug was that it worked too well. Highly recommended!
